transcripts.summary:
- **Survey Enhancements:** 20 features prioritized for April 1 launch, 11 critical, ensuring enough time for testing.

- **Manager Dashboard Changes:** Engagement score label updated to clarify meanings; dashboard displays can switch between graph types.

- **Accurate Invitation Tracking:** 610 managers accepted out of 774 invited, achieving a 78% acceptance rate following data fixes.

- **Exit Survey Limitations:** No branching support; static questions are enforced, with creative matrix questions used to approximate ranking.

- **Communication Coordination:** Single Slack channel established for updates; weekly agendas will track progress and cross-team collaboration.

- **Next Steps:** Framework for Pulse survey to be built from scratch; coordination with Laura for finalized survey structure.

## **Survey Platform Enhancements**

The team prioritized key feature enhancements and agreed on a tight timeline to support the April 1 pulse survey launch.

- **Prioritized 20 Enhancements for April 1 Launch** (03:34)  
    - The team narrowed down initial 30+ requested hover-over enhancements to **11 critical** and some "could have" lower priority items.  
    - Yugi committed to confirming which features engineering will deliver and their timelines by tomorrow or Wednesday.  
    - The goal is to complete development with enough time for testing before the **April 1** rollout.  
    - The prioritization followed a **MoSCoW model**: must-haves for immediate release, could-haves deferred.

- **Multi-Select Filters Across All Heatmap Filters** (06:09)  
    - Accepted enhancement includes multi-select filtering on all heatmap filters, not just L2 fields.  
    - This will improve data slicing for all user personas: managers, sub-admins, and admins.  
    - Consistency in experience across personas was emphasized by Sanya and Scott.

- **Manager Dashboard Adjustments and Reporting Enhancements** (07:48)  
    - Change the engagement score label from "Overall" to "My Team's Overall" to reduce confusion.  
    - Ability to switch dashboard scores over time from line graph to bar graph to improve clarity.  
    - Request to add a company average score column for question-level comparisons remains pending further discussion due to granularity challenges.

- **Expanded Hover-Over Support for Terminology Clarification** (14:15)  
    - Due to platform limitations on changing certain terms, hover-overs will be used to clarify terms like "Org" vs. "Company."  
    - This addresses confusion around organizational levels specific to Yahoo's HR structure.  
    - Sanya will draft hover-over language for review by Catherine before engineering confirmation.

## **Survey Data and Reporting Accuracy**

Significant improvements were made to manager invitation tracking and reporting accuracy.

- **Manager Invitation Counts Fixed and Verified** (22:16)  
    - The issue with incorrect counts of manager invites was resolved, showing accurate numbers of invited, accepted, and not invited managers.  
    - Current data shows **610 managers accepted** out of **774 invited** (after excluding 229 no longer with company), equating to **78% acceptance rate**.  
    - Scott confirmed report export capabilities to verify invitation statuses in real time.

## **Exit Survey Structure and Question Types**

The exit survey requires structural adjustments due to platform limitations on branching and question types.

- **Branching Logic Not Supported in Current Platform** (27:23)  
    - Yugi confirmed that Culture Monkey does not support branching questions based on specific responses; all questions are static.  
    - Scott noted this prevents a direct "lift and shift" of existing exit survey logic.  
    - The team plans to simulate branching with conditional instructions but no dynamic question flow.

- **Stack Ranking vs. Multi-Select Limitations** (28:44)  
    - The platform does not allow limiting the number of selections in multi-select questions.  
    - Stack ranking as a separate feature is not available; instead, matrix questions with selectable ratings (e.g., Very Good to Very Poor) will be used.  
    - Scott requested an example report showing how matrix question data appears on the back end to evaluate suitability.  
    - Yugi agreed to provide a one-pager on survey layout and reporting by tomorrow or Wednesday.

- **Single Select Options for Ranking Questions** (33:54)  
    - Single select questions are supported and can be used as dropdowns for ranking reasons within exit surveys.  
    - The team discussed using matrix questions creatively to approximate stack ranking despite limitations.  
    - Sanya and Scott agreed this approach requires careful design to avoid data quality issues (e.g., users selecting all options equally important).

## **Process and Communication Coordination**

The team established clear communication channels and coordination plans to maintain alignment and meet deadlines.

- **Consolidated Slack Channel for Survey Updates** (00:18)  
    - Charlie announced consolidation of all project channels into a single renamed Slack channel: **Culture Monkey Yahoo Surveys**.  
    - This channel will serve as the main hub for sharing notes and priorities after meetings to ensure alignment.

- **Weekly Agenda and Cross-Team Collaboration** (00:18)  
    - Weekly agendas will continue with Sonia leading poll survey enhancements and Scott handling other survey topics.  
    - Kate and Dana will provide feedback on dashboards and data access when their priorities settle.  
    - Scott proactively compiled a list of analytics features needing training or improvement to prepare for future discussions.

- **Clear Ownership and Timeline Commitments** (20:55)  
    - Yugi committed to delivering a finalized feature list and estimated release dates by **April 1** pulse survey launch, with a max deadline of Wednesday for timelines.  
    - Sanya emphasized including sufficient testing time before rollout.  
    - Charlie and Sanya planned to review hover-over language with Catherine before final confirmation.

- **Next Steps for Survey Framework Setup** (24:57)  
    - Sonia will start building the Pulse survey framework from scratch, following agreed naming conventions to ensure proper sorting and display.  
    - Scott and Sonia will clarify question definitions and back-end access for certain multi-select and branching questions.  
    - Coordination with Laura is planned after finalizing survey structure details.


**Yugi P**
Confirm enhancement delivery timelines and release dates by Wednesday, considering April 1 Pulse survey launch (06:41)
Discuss feasibility with engineering team about adding filter functionality for sub-admins despite dynamic team changes (11:57)
Evaluate hover over label changes from 'Org' to 'Company' and company average score inclusion, revert with feasibility updates by next day (15:40)
Fix and delete test survey data as requested by Scott (24:57)
Provide one-pager/document showing survey layout and corresponding backend report formats for matrix and multi-select questions (33:01)

**Sanya Lamba**
Share updated hover over language drafts for approval before finalization (20:37)
Lead creation of Pulse survey framework from scratch to meet upcoming rollout needs (25:13)

**Scott Domes**
Review and verify manager invite report exports; confirm counts and acceptance metrics with Yugi (23:44)
Assist Sonia in clarifying exit survey multi-select and branching logic limitations (26:36)

**Charlie Trinco**
Draft hover over label text for 'Org' to 'Company' review with Catherine before final submission (20:37)

**Kate Burk**
Review and add feedback on analytic findings for survey projects as time allows (02:08)
